behaviors.description: The keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment when the review contains only words from the configured positive and negative word lists.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is scoped to deterministic analyzer logic (token/keyword counting against configured lists). No real OS/network/subprocess boundary is required, and correctness does not depend on AI/ML output. Rejected higher level: the text says "keyword-frequency analyzer assigns the correct sentiment," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow for this specific check.
behaviors.test_description: Use table-driven unit tests over positive-only, negative-only, and neutral-count inputs with injected word lists and thresholds; assert exact sentiment label outcomes.

behaviors.description: The explanation analyzer produces reasoning that references specific phrases from the original review, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on the model/analyzer’s language judgment quality (grounding explanations in source phrases), which stubs can mask. This is not just wrapper logic; it is output-quality dependent. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one agentic generation step, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real explanation analyzer on a representative review set and assert each explanation includes concrete phrase-level grounding from the input (e.g., extracted spans/quotes) while failing generic-template outputs.

behaviors.description: When a review contains mixed signals (both positive and negative keywords), the sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic rule evaluation in the keyword-frequency analyzer with configuration-driven tie-breaking. No external infra or model output quality is involved. Rejected higher level: behavior text is limited to "sentiment score reflects the configured tie-breaking rule," which is function-level decision logic.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test mixed-keyword inputs around tie boundaries with different configured tie-break rules; assert exact selected sentiment for each configuration.

behaviors.description: The explanation for a sarcastic review (e.g., "Great, another broken feature") correctly identifies the negative intent despite positive surface words.
behaviors.justification: Sarcasm intent detection is inherently model-judgment dependent; deterministic mocks would hide failures. This behavior explicitly tests semantic interpretation quality, not infrastructure around the call. Rejected Workflow: only one model reasoning step is required for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real explanation analyzer on a sarcasm-focused set and check that explanations classify/describe negative intent despite positive lexical cues.

behaviors.description: Both outputs (score and explanation) are returned together in a single response, with the explanation referencing the same sentiment direction as the score.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module orchestration plus semantic consistency with generated explanation content. Integration can verify bundling, but not whether real generated explanations align directionally in practice; mocking the explainer can hide real divergence. Rejected Workflow: sequence does not require multiple model decisions; a single agentic generation must align with deterministic score.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real end-to-end service with real explanation generation enabled; assert one response contains both fields and that explanation sentiment direction matches the returned score across varied inputs.
